tool_name: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis

inputs.name: Feed per Tooth (fz)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Tool Diameter (D)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges (z)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Total Programmed Cutting Length
inputs.hint: Sum of all tool path cutting segments from NC program

inputs.name: Approach + Overtravel per Pass
inputs.hint: Tool entry ramp + exit overtravel — typically 0.5×D to 1×D per pass

inputs.name: Total Rapid (G00) Travel Distance
inputs.hint: Non-cutting repositioning moves — from NC program or simulation

inputs.name: Number of Tool Changes
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Tool-to-Tool Change Time (T-T)
inputs.hint: From machine spec — BT40: 3–6s | HSK63: 2–4s | ATC with arm: 4–8s

inputs.name: Part Load / Unload Time
inputs.hint: Manual: 1–3 min | Pallet changer: 0.1–0.3 min | Robot: 0.2–0.5 min
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Parts per Fixture Setup
inputs.hint: Multi-part fixturing reduces effective cycle time per part
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Machine Hourly Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Include depreciation, maintenance, energy, floor space, operator — typically $40–150/h
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Machine Maximum Spindle Speed
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60034-1:2017 §8.4 — Speed tolerance: ±1% at rated load

formulas: n_rpm = (1000 * cutting_speed_vc) / (3.14159 * tool_diameter_mm)   // [RPM] Required spindle speed
n_actual = MIN(n_rpm, machine_max_rpm)   // [RPM] Actual spindle speed (capped at machine limit)
Vf_feed_rate = feed_per_tooth_fz * num_teeth_z * n_actual   // [mm/min] Table feed rate
T_cut_min = (total_cut_length_mm + approach_overtravel_mm) / Vf_feed_rate   // [min] Pure cutting time
T_rapid_min = rapid_distance_mm / rapid_velocity_mm_min   // [min] Rapid positioning time
T_aux_min = load_unload_time_min + probing_inspection_min   // [min] Auxiliary non-cutting time
T_cycle_gross_min = T_cut_min + T_rapid_min + T_toolchange_min + T_aux_min   // [min] Gross cycle time
T_cycle_per_part_min = T_cycle_gross_min / parts_per_fixture   // [min] Cycle time per part
Cost_per_Part_USD = (T_cycle_per_part_min / 60) * machine_hourly_rate_usd   // [USD] Machining cost per part

tool_diameter_minimum.error_msg: Tool diameter cannot be zero — division by zero in RPM calculation.

rapid_velocity_minimum.error_msg: Rapid traverse rate cannot be zero or unrealistically low. Minimum 100 mm/min.

feed_rate_physical.error_msg: Calculated feed rate exceeds 100,000 mm/min — physically impossible. Check Vc, fz, z, and D inputs.

smart_warnings.message: CRITICAL SPINDLE WASTE: Only cutting for less than 30% of cycle. Machine spends over 70% of time on non-value-adding operations (rapids, ATC, load/unload). Immediate actions: (1) Zero-point clamping system, (2) Pre-set offline tooling, (3) Optimise G00 paths, (4) Pallet changer evaluation.
Low spindle utilisation (30–50%): Significant non-cutting time losses. Target is >65% for general machining. Review ATC time, rapid distances, and fixture strategy.

Tool change time >30% of cutting time: ATC is a major cycle-time driver. Evaluate: (1) Reduce tool count via multi-function tools, (2) Sub-spindle or twin-pallet, (3) Upgrade to faster ATC (arm-type vs. umbrella).

Required RPM exceeds machine maximum. Actual cutting speed reduced. Recalculate with Vc_actual shown. Consider: (1) Smaller tool diameter, (2) Higher-RPM spindle machine, (3) Reduce cutting speed to match machine limit.
Auxiliary time (load/unload + probing) exceeds actual cutting time. Automation of part handling or in-process gauging should be evaluated for ROI.

Machine spindle speed capability underused (<40% of max RPM). Machine may be oversized for this operation, or cutting parameters are conservative. Review tool and material data for speed increase potential.

fmea.effect: Calculated feed rate exceeds 100,000 mm/min — physically impossible. Check Vc, fz, z, and D inputs.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL SPINDLE WASTE: Only cutting for less than 30% of cycle. Machine spends over 70% of time on non-value-adding operations (rapids, ATC, load/unload). Immediate actions: (1) Zero-point clamping system, (2) Pre-set offline tooling, (3) Optimise G00 paths, (4) Pallet changer evaluation.
Low spindle utilisation (30–50%): Significant non-cutting time losses. Target is >65% for general machining. Review ATC time, rapid distances, and fixture strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Spindle_Utilisation_pct >= 30 && Spindle_Utilisation_pct < 50

fmea.failureMode: Tool change time >30% of cutting time: ATC is a major cycle-time driver. Evaluate: (1) Reduce tool count via multi-function tools, (2) Sub-spindle or twin-pallet, (3) Upgrade to faster ATC (arm-type vs. umbrella).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_toolchange_min > T_cut_min * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Required RPM exceeds machine maximum. Actual cutting speed reduced. Recalculate with Vc_actual shown. Consider: (1) Smaller tool diameter, (2) Higher-RPM spindle machine, (3) Reduce cutting speed to match machine limit.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm > machine_max_rpm

fmea.failureMode: Auxiliary time (load/unload + probing) exceeds actual cutting time. Automation of part handling or in-process gauging should be evaluated for ROI.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_aux_min > T_cut_min

fmea.failureMode: Machine spindle speed capability underused (<40% of max RPM). Machine may be oversized for this operation, or cutting parameters are conservative. Review tool and material data for speed increase potential.
